result.summary: Found several visual issues, mainly around overlapping fixed elements and readability of promotional/legal content on the mobile page.

issues.description: The 'SCROLL DOWN' floating prompt overlaps the fine-print terms near the bottom of the promotional section, making the legal copy harder to read.
issues.suggestion: Move the scroll prompt higher or lower, reduce its size, or hide it once the user reaches the terms area so it does not cover content.

issues.description: The fine-print terms are extremely small and low-contrast against the yellow background, making them effectively unreadable on mobile.
issues.suggestion: Increase the font size, improve contrast, and provide a clearly accessible link to full terms and conditions.

issues.description: Some decorative promotional graphics appear clipped at the screen edges, such as the baseball on the right and coins/slot imagery on the left.
issues.suggestion: Adjust the positioning or scale of these assets so they fit cleanly within the mobile viewport, or intentionally crop them in a more polished way.

issues.description: The top BetRivers logo tile in the hero appears visually cut off by the header area, with only the lower portion clearly visible.
issues.suggestion: Add spacing below the fixed header or reposition the hero logo so it is fully visible and not visually crowded.

issues.description: The dense promotional offer text competes visually with multiple badges, bonus amounts, sports/casino imagery, and navigation elements, making the page feel cluttered.
issues.suggestion: Simplify the hierarchy, reduce overlapping decorative elements, and ensure each promotional offer has clear spacing and a distinct call-to-action.
